evals.prompt: I built a Farcaster Frame for NFT minting and want to launch it on LaunchCaster. How do I submit?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step submission guide via Farcaster cast

assertions.name: farcaster_required
assertions.description: Explains that submission requires a Farcaster account and is done by casting with @launch

assertions.name: frame_advantage
assertions.description: Mentions that Frame launches get enhanced display or special treatment on LaunchCaster

assertions.name: optimization_tips
assertions.description: Provides guidance on crafting the cast for maximum engagement (timing, content, visuals)

evals.prompt: Should I launch my DeFi project on LaunchCaster or Product Hunt? What are the tradeoffs?
evals.expected_output: Comparison of both platforms with recommendation

assertions.name: audience_difference
assertions.description: Explains LaunchCaster is Web3-native while Product Hunt serves a general audience

assertions.name: cost_and_process
assertions.description: Notes LaunchCaster is free with no queue while Product Hunt has a competitive 24-hour window

assertions.name: recommendation
assertions.description: Provides a clear recommendation or framework for deciding, considering the DeFi audience fit

evals.prompt: I want to launch a traditional SaaS tool on LaunchCaster — is it worth it?

assertions.name: web3_only
assertions.description: Explains LaunchCaster is exclusively for Web3 projects and traditional SaaS is not a fit

assertions.name: alternatives
assertions.description: Suggests alternative launch directories better suited for SaaS products

assertions.name: routing
assertions.description: Routes to the multi-directory strategy skill or specific platform skills for SaaS launches
